req.title: Export employee directory — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path CSV export with PII redaction for opt-out employees by an hr-admin, (2) access control rejection for non-admins, (3) idempotency under retry, and (4) rate-limiting behaviour. Tests are mapped to EP (valid role / redaction logic), NEG (forbidden role, rate-limit), BVA (single opt-out employee at boundary of directory), STC (retry idempotency state), and DT (role x opt-out decision table). Each test is self-contained via fixtures and placeholders.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_optout_employees
preconditions: Directory contains at least one employee who opted out and at least one who did not
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
Identify rows for opted-out and non-opted-out employees
expected: HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/csv or application/octet-stream
PII fields (email, phone, address) are redacted (e.g. REDACTED) for opted-out employees
PII fields are present and non-empty for non-opted-out employees
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_optout_employees(
    hr_admin_token,        # <fixture> returns valid bearer token for hr-admin
    opted_out_employee_id, # <fixture> seeds and returns id of opted-out employee
    regular_employee_id,   # <fixture> seeds and returns id of non-opted-out employee
    api_client,            # <fixture> returns configured requests.Session
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").lower() or \
           "octet-stream" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").lower()

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {row["id"]: row for row in reader}

    assert str(opted_out_employee_id) in rows, "Opted-out employee missing from CSV"
    assert str(regular_employee_id) in rows, "Regular employee missing from CSV"

    opted_row = rows[str(opted_out_employee_id)]
    regular_row = rows[str(regular_employee_id)]

    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert opted_row.get(pii_field, "").upper() == "REDACTED", \
            f"PII field '{pii_field}' not redacted for opted-out employee"
        assert regular_row.get(pii_field, "") not in ("", "REDACTED"), \
            f"PII field '{pii_field}' incorrectly redacted for non-opted-out employee"

tests.name: test_non_admin_export_csv_returns_403
actions: Authenticate as a standard employee and obtain bearer token

tests.name: test_export_csv_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: Directory state is stable (no writes between calls)
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
expected: No side-effect artefacts (e.g. duplicate rows) in second response
trace: R-WEB-083: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_export_csv_idempotent_under_retry(
    hr_admin_token, # <fixture> returns valid bearer token for hr-admin
    api_client,     # <fixture> returns configured requests.Session
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    resp1 = api_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    resp2 = api_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)

    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    assert resp1.text == resp2.text, \
        "Retry produced a different CSV body — export is not idempotent"

    import csv, io
    rows1 = list(csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp1.text)))
    rows2 = list(csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp2.text)))
    assert len(rows1) == len(rows2), \
        "Second call returned different row count — possible duplicate side-effect"

tests.name: test_export_csv_single_optout_employee_boundary
preconditions: Directory contains exactly one employee and that employee opted out
actions: Seed directory with exactly one opted-out employee via <seed_single_optout_employee>
Parse CSV and inspect the single data row
expected: CSV contains exactly one data row (plus header)
All PII fields in that row are REDACTED
Non-PII fields (e.g. employee_id, department) are present and correct
trace: R-WEB-083: BVA — boundary of directory size = 1
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

def test_export_csv_single_optout_employee_boundary(
    hr_admin_token,            # <fixture> returns valid bearer token for hr-admin
    api_client,                # <fixture> returns configured requests.Session
    seed_single_optout_employee, # <fixture> seeds exactly one opted-out employee, returns its id
):
    employee_id = seed_single_optout_employee
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = list(reader)
    assert len(rows) == 1, f"Expected 1 data row, got {len(rows)}"

    row = rows[0]
    assert str(row.get("id", "")) == str(employee_id)
    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert row.get(pii_field, "").upper() == "REDACTED", \
            f"PII field '{pii_field}' not redacted in single-employee boundary case"
    assert row.get("department", "") not in ("", "REDACTED"), \
        "Non-PII field 'department' should not be redacted"

tests.name: test_rate_limited_export_returns_429
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold for /api/employees/export.csv is known (N requests per window)
actions: Send N+1 rapid sequential GET /api/employees/export.csv requests
Capture status codes of all responses
expected: At least one response (N+1 or earlier) returns HTTP 429
429 response includes Retry-After header or rate-limit error body
trace: DT rule: role=hr-admin AND requests > limit => 429
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = 10  # <config> replace with actual per-window limit

def test_rate_limited_export_returns_429(
    hr_admin_token, # <fixture> returns valid bearer token for hr-admin
    api_client,     # <fixture> returns configured requests.Session
    reset_rate_limit, # <fixture> clears rate-limit counter before test
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    statuses = []
    for _ in range(RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1):
        r = api_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
        statuses.append(r.status_code)
        if r.status_code == 429:
            assert r.headers.get("Retry-After") or r.text, \
                "429 must include Retry-After header or error body"
            break
    assert 429 in statuses, \
        f"Expected 429 after {RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD} requests; got: {statuses}"
